sections.heading: Eligibility for adults (18 and above)
items: Learn about the eligibility criteria for adult applicants. You can also check your eligibility by answering some questions .
To be a Canadian citizen as an adult, you must:

sections.heading: have enough physical presence or time lived in Canada
have filed income taxes in Canada if needed
prove your language skills in English or French (adults 18 to 54 only)
pass the citizenship test (adults 18 to 54 only)
Eligibility for minors (under 18)
items: Depending on their parents, a minor can apply through the minor 5(2) or 5(1) process.
bullets: Minor 5(2) You need to apply under this process if the minor has a Canadian parent or a parent who is applying for citizenship at the same time To apply for the minor, you must be their parent (biological or adoptive) or legal guardian Check all of the requirements for minor 5(2)
a Canadian parent or
a parent who is applying for citizenship at the same time
parent (biological or adoptive) or
legal guardian

Minor 5(1) You need to apply under this process if the minor doesn’t have a Canadian parent or a parent who is applying for citizenship at the same time To apply for the minor, you must be someone who has custody of the minor, or can legally act on their behalf don’t have to be a Canadian citizen A minor may apply on their own if there’s no one who can apply for them They need to select the waiver checkbox in the signature section of their online or paper application. By selecting this checkbox, they’re requesting to apply alone. Check all of the requirements for minor 5(1)
must be someone who has custody of the minor, or can legally act on their behalf
don’t have to be a Canadian citizen

sections.heading: Accessibility options and exemptions
items: If you need assistance to meet certain requirements for accessibility reasons (like a disability), you can request:
Find the option that’s right for you:
bullets: help to meet a requirement (an accommodation ) or
permission to skip a requirement that you can’t meet (a waiver )

sections.heading: Spouses of Canadian citizens
items: You don’t automatically become a citizen when you marry a Canadian.
If you’re the spouse of a Canadian citizen, you must meet the same requirements for adults or minors above.

sections.heading: Children and grandchildren of Canadian citizens
items: If you have a Canadian parent or grandparent, you may be a Canadian citizen.
bullets: Check if you may already be a Canadian citizen
To find out for sure , apply for a Canadian citizenship certificate

sections.heading: Possible loss of current citizenship
items: Canada allows dual citizenship, but not every country allows this.
bullets: You may lose your current citizenship if you become a Canadian citizen.
If you’re not sure, contact the embassy, high commission or consulate of your current country of citizenship.
